body:
This PR includes several improvements to the PDF generation and rendering system:

- Fix canvas assignment logic to only unassign when space is needed
- Fix PDF regeneration with lightweight update without webview flash
- Fix menu highlighting to separate default items (📝) from selected items (✓)
- Fix font size persistence and webview updates
- Add header/footer color constant and make it lighter
- Fix missing header/footer on page 1
- Refactor PDF generation to use unified jsPDF approach
- Clean up obsolete methods and improve VSCodeAPIs constants
- Fix coordinate system: jsPDF uses top-left origin, not bottom-left

This builds on the previous multi-page rendering work and includes various bug fixes and improvements.

## Walkthrough

Major refactor migrating PDF rendering from token/page-based generation to a unified line-by-line pipeline and a single rendering lifecycle. Adds a new Coords utility for unit/coordinate conversions and exposes DocInfo_PDF (now wrapping jsPDF and renaming margin fields to topMarginPts, bottomMarginPts, leftMarginPts, rightMarginPts). PDF flow now uses setupPdf(...), incremental per-line rendering via renderByLine / renderContent, and finishPdf() to produce DocInfo_PDF; per-page extraction is via renderContent(pageNumber, lineBegin, lineEnd, options). PaperPrinter, UIScrollView, UIWebView, types, and templates updated to the new RenderOptions and PageRender signatures. Diagnostics message formatting and truncation state added (Diagnostics.reset()), menu selection APIs and webview panel typing changed, scroll-view placeholder/canvas lifecycle reworked, and some tests were removed/renamed.

## Sequence Diagram(s)

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant PaperPrinter as PP
    participant Stylize
    participant PDF
    participant Coords
    participant DocInfo as DocInfo_PDF
    participant Webview

    User->>PP: request generatePdf(code, options)
    activate PP

    PP->>Stylize: tokenize(code)
    Stylize-->>PP: lines

    PP->>PDF: setupPdf(options)
    activate PDF
    PDF->>Coords: init()
    PDF->>DocInfo: create jsPDF wrapper
    deactivate PDF

    loop per-line rendering
      PP->>PDF: renderByLine(line, callback)
      activate PDF
      PDF->>Coords: convert coords / metrics
      PDF->>DocInfo: draw line
      alt line causes page break
        PDF->>PDF: addHeaderAndFooter()
        PDF->>DocInfo: newPage()
      end
      deactivate PDF
    end

    PP->>PDF: finishPdf()
    activate PDF
    PDF->>PDF: renderPageTotals()
    PDF->>DocInfo: finalize and return DocInfo_PDF
    PDF->>Coords: done()
    deactivate PDF

    Note right of Webview: on-demand page rendering
    Webview->>PDF: renderContent(pageNumber, lineBegin, lineEnd, options)
    PDF-->>Webview: PageData (rendered page / canvas)
```

## Possibly related PRs

- appliedmedia/print2paper4vscode#24 — Directly implements the same shift to a line-by-line rendering pipeline (renderByLine/finish) and matches PageRender signature changes.  
- appliedmedia/print2paper4vscode#20 — Alters PaperPrinter regeneration and generatePdf orchestration to the DocInfo_PDF-centered flow (regenerateAndUpdateWebview).  
- appliedmedia/print2paper4vscode#21 — Refactors around DocInfo_PDF-driven full-document and per-page rendering and adjusts the public PDF API surface.

🤖 @cursoragent: ✅ Fixed it! Commit [a9bd31c] - PDF.ts renderContent caching: Added page caching using existing pageCache Map to avoid re-serializing entire PDF on every call.

🤖 @cursoragent: ✅ Fixed it! Commit [a9bd31c] - PDF.ts coordinate logs contradiction: Removed contradictory log statement that claimed 'Y=0 at bottom, Y increases upward' when jsPDF uses top-left origin.

🤖 @cursoragent: 🚫 Won't do because the regex is working correctly for the current use case and changing it would require extensive testing. The current implementation captures color values properly from the HTML spans generated by Shiki.
